author: Nintendo Everything
text:
3D platformer Garfield: Escape from Monday announced for Nintendo Switch [update: trailer]

May 13: Microids, along with OSome Studio, today unveiled Garfield: Escape from Monday for Nintendo Switch. The game is due out on September 24, 2026. Garfield: Escape from Monday is a “veggie filled” 3D platformer. In it, players will need to help the tabby cat wake up from his nightmare by exploring different worlds. More information can be found in...

The post 3D platformer Garfield: Escape from Monday announced for Nintendo Switch [update: trailer] appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Pokemon Pokopia 1.1.1 update out now, patch notes

Pokemon Pokopia can now download the game’s latest update that just went live, which is version 1.1.1. According to Nintendo, a couple of issues have been fixed. These involved placing items and using an emote while taking photos with the camera. Thanks to Nintendo, the full patch notes are as follows: Pokemon Pokopia version 1.1.1 update patch notes The following...

text:
Nintendo Music – list of all games included

For those looking for a complete list of all games included and supported in Nintendo Music, that’s where this post comes in. Nintendo Music only came with a few songs at launch. However, since then, a few more have been added. Nintendo has also indicated that additional music is coming in the future. Currently it seems that we can expect...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
The winners and losers of Pokemon Champions’ Regulation M-B

Pokemon Champions’ newest format, Regulation M-B, has finally begun! At first, we thought Mega Raichu X and Mega Raichu Y would be the only new additions, but it turns out that all sorts of other new Pokemon and Mega Evolutions have been added to the game as well. Additionally, some of these new Pokemon look to be rather strong –...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Moonlight Pale confirmed for Nintendo Switch

According to LightWitch and Blue Lily’s latest announcement, Moonlight Pale is making its way to Nintendo Switch. The team aims to release its game between Q4 2026 and Q1 2027. Moonlight Pale, which is currently on Kickstarter, is a hand-drawn survival horror game set in a late 1800s female seminary. You’ll play as Juliette, “a student at St. Birgitta’s female...

The post Moonlight Pale confirmed for Nintendo Switch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Shinobi: Art of Vengeance coming to Nintendo Switch 2

SEGA is putting Shinobi: Art of Vengeance on Nintendo Switch 2, the company just announced. Fans will be able to grab this version on September 24, 2026. Shinobi: Art of Vengeance previously came to Nintendo Switch. However, on the new console, it’ll feature an improved resolution. For those interested in the game, check out the following details: The iconic Shinobi...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Arcade game Biomechanical Toy to hit Nintendo Switch

QUByte Interactive made it known today that it’s working on an arcade port of Biomechanical Toy for Nintendo Switch. It’ll be out on July 16, 2026. Gaelco was behind the original debut of Biomechanical Toy in 1995. For this release, fans can look forward to rewind functionality and save states plus the ability to view the original artwork and access...

The post Arcade game Biomechanical Toy to hit Nintendo Switch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Glover, Street Racer Collection getting free Nintendo Switch updates to add PS1 version

As announced by QUByte today, Glover and Street Racer Collection will both be receiving free updates on Nintendo Switch to add their PlayStation versions. The two will have their patches arrive in 2027. Glover on the PlayStation 1 came out after the N64 version. There were quite a few changes on PS1 because the hardware was less powerful. It resulted...

The post Glover, Street Racer Collection getting free Nintendo Switch updates to add PS1 version appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
PS1 games Invasion from Beyond, Motor Mash, Tigershark coming to Nintendo Switch

Today, QUByte Interactive unveiled a trio of future PlayStation 1 games releasing on Nintendo Switch. Invasion from Beyond, Motor Mash, and Tigershark are all lined up for a 2027 launch. Invasion from Beyond, also known as B-Movie, initially came out in 1998. The same goes for Motor Mash. Tigershark is the earliest of the bunch, as its original launch took...

The post PS1 games Invasion from Beyond, Motor Mash, Tigershark coming to Nintendo Switch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Gaelco Sports Collection, Thunder Hoop Collection announced for Nintendo Switch

QUByte Interactive is making Gaelco Sports Collection and Thunder Hoop Collection for Nintendo Switch, the company revealed today. A release is scheduled for 2026 both both packages. Gaelco Sports Collection brings together five arcade titles that launched between 1992 and 1995. Squash, World Rally 1 and 2, Touch and Go, and Snowboarding Champions make up the lineup. Thunder Hoop Collection...

The post Gaelco Sports Collection, Thunder Hoop Collection announced for Nintendo Switch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.
